title: Analysis of Breast Cancer Cell Invasion Using an Organotypic Culture System
author: Romana E. Ranftl
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bn6jmhcn

steps.title: All components of the gel mix...
steps.instruction: All components of the gel mix must be kept on ice. Here we describe the protocol for 1 mL gel mix containing fibroblasts or without fibroblasts. This is the volume required for one sample in one well of a 24-well plate. The total volume of gel mix needs to be scaled up according to sample size. Generally, it is recommended to prepare gel in excess to avoid pipetting errors due to the viscous nature of the components.

steps.title: Mix 100 µL, FBS , 80...
steps.instruction: Mix 100 µL, FBS , 80 µL, 5× DMEM , and 120 µL, fibroblast culture medium .

steps.title: Add 200 µL, Matrigel® and 400...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, Matrigel® and 400 µL, collagen I ( see Notes 10 and 11 ). Keep the mixture On ice ( see Note 12 ).

steps.title: Trypsinize fibroblasts of interest (NFs/CAFs) from...
steps.instruction: Trypsinize fibroblasts of interest (NFs/CAFs) from a monolayer to a single cell suspension. Count the cells, centrifuge 400 x g, °C, 5 minutes , and resuspend the pellet at a concentration of 10 7 cells/mL in fibroblast culture medium ( see Notes 13 and 14 ).

steps.title: Avoiding bubble formation, add 100 µL,...
steps.instruction: Avoiding bubble formation, add 100 µL, cell suspension to the gel mix. If a gel without fibroblasts is required, add 100 µL, fibroblast culture medium instead of cell suspension ( see Note 15 ).

steps.title: Add 900 µL of the mixture...
steps.instruction: Add 900 µL of the mixture in a 24 well-plate well (Fig. 2c) ( see Note 16 ).

steps.title: Place the plate at 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Place the plate at 37 °C and 5% CO 2 for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: When the gel is set, add...
steps.instruction: When the gel is set, add 1 mL of appropriate medium on top and incubate Overnight at 37 °C and 5% CO 2 ( see Note 17 ).

steps.title: Depending on the number and matrix-remodeling...
steps.instruction: Depending on the number and matrix-remodeling ability of the fibroblasts, some of the gels may have already contracted at this point. The plate can be scanned to document this, as gel contraction can serve as an additional read-out of CAF function (Fig. 2d).

steps.title: Aspirate the medium carefully from the...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the medium carefully from the 24-well plate containing the gels.

steps.title: Apply 100 µL, cancer cell suspension...
steps.instruction: Apply 100 µL, cancer cell suspension on the top of each of the gels ( see Notes 18 and 19 ).

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C and 5% CO 2
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C and 5% CO 2 . Leave the cells to adhere to the matrix for 06:00:00 – 08:00:00 .

steps.title: Given the softness of the gels,...
steps.instruction: Given the softness of the gels, direct contact with the metal can damage the gels. Before placing the gels on the bridges, nylon filters have to be prepared and placed in-between the gels and the metal grid.

steps.title: Prepare 1 mL, gel without fibroblasts...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1 mL, gel without fibroblasts according to the recipe described in Section 3.1 "Gel Preparation."

steps.title: Coat the nylon filters using 1 mL, cell-free gel (Fig
steps.instruction: Coat the nylon filters using 1 mL, cell-free gel (Fig. 2e).

steps.title: Separate the coated filters in the culture dish (Fig
steps.instruction: Separate the coated filters in the culture dish (Fig. 2f).

steps.title: Incubate the coated nylon filters at 37 °C for 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the coated nylon filters at 37 °C for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Fix the coated nylon, using filter...
steps.instruction: Fix the coated nylon, using filter fixing solution for at least 02:00:00 at Room temperature or Overnight at 4 °C .

steps.title: The gels are placed on the...
steps.instruction: The gels are placed on the metal bridges and medium is fed from underneath to impose a direction of invasion. The breast cancer cell layer is covered with a thin layer of gel for improved simulation of in vivo conditions ( see Note 20 ).

steps.title: Add cancer cell culture medium to...
steps.instruction: Add cancer cell culture medium to the filters and incubate for 00:30:00 at 37 °C .

steps.title: Place the sterile metal grid bridges...
steps.instruction: Place the sterile metal grid bridges in a 6-well plate with sterile forceps (Fig. 2g).

steps.title: Add a coated, washed and medium-adapted...
steps.instruction: Add a coated, washed and medium-adapted nylon filter on top of each metal bridge with sterile forceps (Fig. 2h).

steps.title: Prepare an appropriate amount of cell-free...
steps.instruction: Prepare an appropriate amount of cell-free gel according to the recipe in Section "Gel Preparation" and keep the mixture On ice ( 100 µL per organotypic gel).

steps.title: Remove the medium on the top...
steps.instruction: Remove the medium on the top of the gels carefully in order not to disturb the organotypic cultures.

steps.title: Lift the gels with a sterile...
steps.instruction: Lift the gels with a sterile spatula from the 24-well and place it on a coated filter on top of a metal bridge. The cancer cell layer must be facing up (Fig. 2i, j).

steps.title: Add medium under the bridge until...
steps.instruction: Add medium under the bridge until it is in contact with the nylon filter. Avoid air bubbles between the interphase medium/nylon filter (Fig. 2k).

steps.title: Add 100 µL, cell-free gel mixture...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL, cell-free gel mixture on top of the lifted gels and spread over the surface.

steps.title: Incubate the culture for 5 days...
steps.instruction: Incubate the culture for 5 days ( 37 °C , 5% CO 2 ) and change the medium daily ( see Note 21 ).

steps.title: After culturing for 5 days (...
steps.instruction: After culturing for 5 days ( see Note 9 ), remove the medium from the plate and wash with PBS or transfer the organotypic cultures to a new 6-well plate by lifting the filter and gel. Turn the gel upside-down for fixing (cancer cell layer on the bottom, Fig. 1) ( see Note 22 ).

steps.title: Fix the gels in 2 mL,...
steps.instruction: Fix the gels in 2 mL, gel fixing solution at 4 °C Overnight .

steps.title: Next day, wash the gels with...
steps.instruction: Next day, wash the gels with 2 mL, PBS for 00:10:00 (×3).

steps.title: Using the forceps and the scalpel...
steps.instruction: Using the forceps and the scalpel cut the gel in two halves and store one of them in 70% ethanol at 4 °C ( see Note 23 ).

steps.title: Embed the other half of the...
steps.instruction: Embed the other half of the gel in paraffin blocks and perform standard H&E staining on sections ( see Notes 24 and 25 ).

steps.title: Take 5–7 pictures per gel using...
steps.instruction: Take 5–7 pictures per gel using a bright field microscope (20×/10× magnification) ( see Note 26 and Fig. 3a-e). Fig. 3 Analysis of cancer cell invasion using carcinoma cells in an organotypic invasion assay. ( a ) Representative image of H&E staining of an organotypic invasion assay of 4T1 cells cultured in the presence of CAFs. The different parts of the organotypic gel are indicated. Right panel is a zoom up region that shows both collective and single cell invasion of 4T1 cells. Fibroblasts and ECM fibers are also observed. ( b ) Representative images of H&E staining of organotypic invasion assays of 4T1 murine breast cancer cells cultured in the absence and presence of either murine normal fibroblasts (NFs) or PyMT-CAFs. Invasion indexes of 4T1 cells for each experimental setup are also indicated. Scale bar, 50 μm. ( c ) Representative images of H&E staining of “killing” organotypic invasion assays of 4T1 murine breast cancer cells cultured in gels previously remodeled by murine normal fibroblasts, PyMT-CAFs or mock-remodeled (no fibroblasts). Invasion indexes of 4T1 cells for each experimental set-up are also indicated. Scale bar, 50 μm. ( d ) Representative images of H&E staining of organotypic invasion assays of MDA-MB-231 human breast cancer cells cultured in the absence and presence of human breast cancer CAFs . The invasion index is indicated. Scale bar, 50 μm. ( e ) Representative images of H&E staining of organotypic invasion assays of 410.4 murine breast cancer cells with murine NFs and CAFs . Note that 410.4 cells present a “collective” mode of invasion. Scale bar, 50 μm. ( f ) Exemplars for image analysis of a representative image of H&E staining of an organotypic invasion assay of 4T1 cells cultured in the presence of CAFs (shown in panel a ). First, the non-invaded area ( red ) and the total area ( blue ) are measured using Image J ( http://imagej.nih.gov/ij/ ). The invading area is then calculated by subtracting the non-invading area from the total area. The invasion index is the ratio of invaded area vs. total area. Invasion index and areas are also indicated

steps.title: Measure the area of noninvasive cancer...
steps.instruction: Measure the area of noninvasive cancer cells and the total area of cancer cells (noninvasive and invasive) using Image J or a similar software (Fig. 3f).

steps.title: The Invasion Index is calculated by...
steps.instruction: The Invasion Index is calculated by dividing the invading area (total area − invading area) by the total area ( see Note 27 ).

steps.title: CAFs have been shown to form...
steps.instruction: CAFs have been shown to form tracks in the ECM , which has been associated with increased invasiveness of cancer cells [10]. However, CAFs can also produce soluble factors that can promote cancer cell invasion [8]. The basic organotypic invasion assay described above does not allow discriminating between these two abilities. In order to specifically study the capacity of CAFs to form tracks in the gel that allow subsequent cancer cell invasion, as well as the potential of cancer cells to invade a gel previously remodeled by CAFs, a variation of the basic organotypic culture assay can be performed: the “killing” assay . Fibroblasts are seeded as described earlier and are allowed to remodel the gel matrix for 5 days before they are removed. When cancer cells are seeded on top of the gel, they will invade into the tracks formed by the fibroblasts (Fig.3c). This modified version also allows for discriminating the selective effect of chemical compounds on fibroblasts/cancer cells as both compartments are never cultured together.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C , 5%...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C , 5% CO 2 for 5 days. Change the medium daily ( see Notes 17 and 28 ).

steps.title: Fibroblast removal (“killing” step)
steps.instruction: Fibroblast removal (“killing” step): Remove the medium from the gels and add 1 mL, fibroblast culture medium containing an appropriate selection compound (e.g., 10 μg/mL puromycin) ( see Note 29 ).

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C , 5% CO 2 for 48:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C , 5% CO 2 for 48:00:00 .

steps.title: Wash the gels for 01:00:00 in fibroblast culture medium (×3)
steps.instruction: Wash the gels for 01:00:00 in fibroblast culture medium (×3).

steps.title: Continue with the procedure as described in Sections 3
steps.instruction: Continue with the procedure as described in Sections 3.2 "Breast Cancer Cell Preparation" – 3.6 "Data Analysis".

steps.title: Trypsinize a monolayer of breast carcinoma cells (4T1, 410
steps.instruction: Trypsinize a monolayer of breast carcinoma cells (4T1, 410.4 or MBA-MD-231), count the cells and prepare a single cell suspension at 5 × 10 6 cells/mL in cancer cell culture medium.

steps.title: Prepare the adequate number of nylon...
steps.instruction: Prepare the adequate number of nylon filters (1 per organotypic condition) with a sterile forceps on top of each other in a culture dish.

steps.title: Wash the gel-coated nylon filters with...
steps.instruction: Wash the gel-coated nylon filters with sterile PBS for 00:10:00 (×3) to remove all traces of fixing solution.

steps.title: Follow the protocol as described in Section 3
steps.instruction: Follow the protocol as described in Section 3.1 "Gel Preparation" ( steps 2 –9). Once the gel is set, add 1 mL, fibroblast culture medium on top. go to step #2

steps.title: Prechill all components for the gel preparation On ice
steps.instruction: Prechill all components for the gel preparation On ice .

steps.title: Wash the gel-coated nylon filters with...
steps.instruction: Wash the gel-coated nylon filters with sterile PBS for 00:10:00 (1/3)

steps.title: Wash the gel-coated nylon filters with...
steps.instruction: Wash the gel-coated nylon filters with sterile PBS for 00:10:00 (2/3)

steps.title: Wash the gel-coated nylon filters with...
steps.instruction: Wash the gel-coated nylon filters with sterile PBS for 00:10:00 (3/3)

steps.title: Wash the gels with 2 mL, PBS for 00:10:00 (1/3)
steps.instruction: Wash the gels with 2 mL, PBS for 00:10:00 (1/3)

steps.title: Wash the gels with 2 mL, PBS for 00:10:00 (2/3)
steps.instruction: Wash the gels with 2 mL, PBS for 00:10:00 (2/3)

steps.title: Wash the gels with 2 mL, PBS for 00:10:00 (3/3)
steps.instruction: Wash the gels with 2 mL, PBS for 00:10:00 (3/3)

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C , 5%...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C , 5% CO 2 Overnight in fibroblast culture medium to remove all traces of selection compound ( see Note 30 ).

steps.title: Wash the gels for 01:00:00 in fibroblast culture medium (1/3)
steps.instruction: Wash the gels for 01:00:00 in fibroblast culture medium (1/3)

steps.title: Wash the gels for 01:00:00 in fibroblast culture medium (3/3)
steps.instruction: Wash the gels for 01:00:00 in fibroblast culture medium (3/3)

steps.title: Wash the gels for 01:00:00 in fibroblast culture medium (2/3)
steps.instruction: Wash the gels for 01:00:00 in fibroblast culture medium (2/3)
